title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Protocol Clinic: Net Certification Evidence
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net certification evidence increment in a performance-based branch network capstone. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
Solve performance-based configuration and troubleshooting tasks by preserving requirements, recording state, and verifying all paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Certification Evidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Net Certification Evidence contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against n10-009 objectives.

content.text: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Certification Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness. This fails the Net Certification Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Net Certification Evidence, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Build Net Certification Evidence evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-certification-evidence-1y0uo2" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Certification Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-certification-evidence-1y0uo2
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Net Certification Evidence model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Certification Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
